ocr.text: In recent years, the European construction sector has faced fluctuating trends in housing starts and permits, reflecting broader
economic conditions and market demands. The data table illustrates notable developments, particularly in the residential
segment, where the total value for 2022 reached EUR 30.0 million, indicating a robust recovery trajectory. The year-on-year
change (YoYChange(%)) for residential housing starts was an impressive 170,000, underscoring heightened activity in this
sector. Furthermore, the overall number of permits issued in 2020 was characterized by a significant volume, as highlighted
under the "Permits" column, contributing to the foundation for future construction projects. Notably, the industrial sector also
demonstrated resilience, with its reporting for 2020 reflecting a stable baseline for growth.

By 2022, residential housing starts surged to a total of 160,000, reinforcing the upward trend observed in previous years. In the
broader context of infrastructure development, it is essential to acknowledge that the market share held by this segment stood at
9.38%, indicating its critical role in the overall construction landscape. The year-on-year change for residential permits, as
depicted in the table, was noted at 130,000, which further emphasizes the dynamic shifts occurring within the market.
Collectively, these metrics provide a comprehensive overview of the evolving housing landscape in Europe, facilitating
informed decision-making for stakeholders within the construction and building materials industry.
